poster_title: Seeing, Listening, Remembering, and Reasoning: A Multimodal Agent with Long-Term Memory
authors: Lin Long, Yichen He, Wentao Ye, Yiyuan Pan, Yuan Lin, Hang Li, Junbo Zhao, Wei Li

content: Multimodal agents require continuous perception through sensors.
Effective long-term memory is vital for world knowledge retention.
Reasoning over long-term memory guides agent actions.
Current methods focus on finite offline videos, not continuous online streams.
Traditional video descriptions lack high-level world knowledge, leading to ambiguity.
M3-Agent processes real-time visual and auditory inputs.
Episodic memory captures concrete events; semantic memory captures general knowledge.
Entity-centric memory structure ensures coherence and consistency.
Evaluated on M3-Bench, a new LVQA benchmark.
M3-Bench includes 100 robot-perspective and 920 web-sourced videos.
Outperforms baselines by 6.7%, 7.7%, and 5.3% on M3-Bench-robot, M3-Bench-web, and VideoMME-long.
Ablation studies show significant drops when key components are removed.
M3-Agent excels in multimodal reasoning with long-term memory.
M3-Agent advances multimodal agents toward human-like long-term memory.
Provides insights for practical design of multimodal agents with long-term memory.
